---
title: "varslice"
subtitle: "Slicing gridded netCDF variables in space and time"
---

## Overview

`varslice` loads and slices gridded variables (up to four dimensions: `x`, `y`,
`z`, `time`) out of one or more netCDF files. It is the gridded cousin of the
lightweight [series](series.qmd) module: where `series` handles 0-D time series,
`varslice` handles full spatial fields that evolve over time.

The problem it solves is reading a *slice* of a large field without holding the
whole thing in memory or writing bespoke `nc_read` calls at every call site:

- **Time slicing** — given a target time (or time range), it reads only the
  required timesteps from disk and reduces them to the requested output via a
  configurable `slice_method` (exact match, range, interpolation, extrapolation,
  or a range statistic such as mean/min/max/standard deviation).
- **Sub-annual (subgrid) time** — a variable may carry a fractional sub-axis
  (e.g. 12 monthly values per year). `varslice` builds the sub-annual time axis
  and lets a range statistic repeat over it (e.g. produce a 12-month climatology
  by applying a mean to every 12th index).
- **Multi-file matching** — a single `filename` may be a glob pattern spanning
  many files; `varslice` expands it, concatenates the time dimension across
  files, and reads a contiguous span transparently.
- **Unit handling** — an input variable is rescaled on read via `unit_scale`
  and `unit_offset`, with missing values preserved.

The variable is configured once (from a namelist or from arguments), then
updated repeatedly to whatever time the calling model currently needs.

## Derived types

### `varslice_param_class`

Static configuration for one variable, mostly loaded from the namelist.

| Component | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `filename` | `character(len=1024)` | Source file or glob pattern (may contain `{domain}` / `{grid_name}`) |
| `filenames(:)` | `character(len=1024)`, alloc. | Expanded list of matching files |
| `name` | `character(len=56)` | netCDF variable name |
| `units_in` | `character(len=56)` | Units in the file (metadata) |
| `units_out` | `character(len=56)` | Units after scaling (metadata) |
| `unit_scale` | `real(wp)` | Multiplicative factor applied on read |
| `unit_offset` | `real(wp)` | Additive offset applied on read |
| `with_time` | `logical` | Whether the variable has a time dimension |
| `with_time_sub` | `logical` | Whether a sub-annual axis is used (derived from `time_par(4)`) |
| `ndim` | `integer` | Number of dimensions of the variable (set on init) |
| `time_par(4)` | `real(dp)` | Time-axis definition `[x0, x1, dx, n_sub]` |

### `varslice_class`

The main object: parameters plus the loaded coordinates, time axis and data.

| Component | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `par` | `varslice_param_class` | Configuration (above) |
| `time_range(2)` | `real(dp)` | Current requested time range (set by update) |
| `slice_method` | `character(len=56)` | Current slice method (set by update) |
| `range_rep` | `integer` | Current repetition frequency (set by update) |
| `dim(:)` | `integer`, alloc. | Size of each dimension of the variable |
| `x(:)`, `y(:)`, `z(:)` | `real(wp)`, alloc. | Spatial coordinate axes |
| `time(:)`, `time_sub(:)` | `real(dp)`/`real(wp)`, alloc. | Full time axis and sub-annual index |
| `idx(:)` | `integer`, alloc. | Indices selected on the last update |
| `var(:,:,:,:)` | `real(wp)`, alloc. | The sliced data field `(x,y,z,time)` |

`var` is always allocated rank-4; unused trailing dimensions have size 1.

## Public API

| Procedure | Description |
|---|---|
| `varslice_init_nml` | Initialize a variable from a namelist group |
| `varslice_init_arg` | Initialize a variable from explicit arguments |
| `varslice_init_data` | (Re)load coordinates and time axis for an already-configured variable |
| `varslice_update` | Read/slice the field to match a given time or time range |
| `varslice_map_to_grid` | Remap a `varslice` field from a source grid to a target grid |
| `varslice_end` | Deallocate all data arrays in the object |
| `get_matching_files` | Expand a glob pattern into a sorted list of filenames |
| `print_var_range` | Print the min/max of a field (skipping missing values) |

The types `varslice_param_class` and `varslice_class` are also public.

### Signatures

```fortran
! Initialize from a namelist group
subroutine varslice_init_nml(vs, filename, group, domain, grid_name, verbose)
    type(varslice_class), intent(INOUT) :: vs
    character(len=*),     intent(IN)    :: filename        ! namelist file
    character(len=*),     intent(IN)    :: group           ! namelist group name
    character(len=*),     intent(IN), optional :: domain    ! substitutes {domain}
    character(len=*),     intent(IN), optional :: grid_name ! substitutes {grid_name}
    logical,              intent(IN), optional :: verbose

! Initialize from explicit arguments (no namelist)
subroutine varslice_init_arg(vs, filename, name, units_in, units_out, &
                             scale, offset, with_time, time_par)
    type(varslice_class), intent(INOUT) :: vs
    character(len=*),     intent(IN)    :: filename, name, units_in, units_out
    real(wp), optional,   intent(IN)    :: scale, offset
    logical,  optional,   intent(IN)    :: with_time
    real(wp), optional,   intent(IN)    :: time_par(:)

! Update the field to a given time or time range
subroutine varslice_update(vs, time, method, fill, rep, print_summary)
    type(varslice_class),       intent(INOUT) :: vs
    real(wp),         optional, intent(IN)    :: time(:)       ! time or [t0,t1]
    character(len=*), optional, intent(IN)    :: method        ! slice_method
    character(len=*), optional, intent(IN)    :: fill          ! only "none" supported
    integer,          optional, intent(IN)    :: rep           ! repetition frequency
    logical,          optional, intent(IN)    :: print_summary
```

**`method` (slice_method)** selects how the read timesteps are reduced:

- `"exact"` — the timestep matching `time` exactly (missing if unavailable).
- `"range"` — all timesteps within `[t0,t1]`, kept along the time dimension.
- `"interp"` — linear interpolation to `time` from the two bracketing
  timesteps; missing if `time` is out of bounds.
- `"extrap"` — like `"interp"`, but clamps to the first/last timestep when out
  of bounds.
- `"range_mean"`, `"range_min"`, `"range_max"`, `"range_sd"`, `"range_sum"` —
  a single slice computed as the mean / min / max / standard deviation / sum
  over `[t0,t1]`.

**`rep`** (stored as `range_rep`) is the frequency at which a range method is
applied. `rep=1` reduces the whole range to one value; `rep=12` applies the
statistic to every 12th index, yielding 12 values along the time dimension
(e.g. a monthly climatology).

**`fill`** currently only accepts `"none"`; other values abort (not yet
implemented).

## Namelist configuration

`varslice_init_nml` reads the following keys from the given group (via
`nml_read` in `varslice_par_load`):

| Key | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `filename` | string | Source file or glob pattern; supports `{domain}` and `{grid_name}` placeholders |
| `name` | string | netCDF variable name |
| `units_in` | string | Units in the file (metadata only) |
| `units_out` | string | Units after scaling (metadata only) |
| `unit_scale` | real | Multiplicative factor applied on read |
| `unit_offset` | real | Additive offset applied on read |
| `with_time` | logical | `.TRUE.` if the variable has a time dimension |
| `time_par` | real(4) | Time axis `[x0, x1, dx, n_sub]` |

Notes on `time_par = [x0, x1, dx, n_sub]`:

- `x0`, `x1`, `dx` define the file's time axis; the generated axis must match
  the netCDF time length or init aborts.
- If `dx == 0`, `x1` is forced to `x0` (single timestep).
- If `n_sub > 1`, a sub-annual axis is built with `n_sub` steps per major unit
  (e.g. `n_sub = 12` for monthly data) and `with_time_sub` is set `.TRUE.`.

Example namelist group:

```fortran
&ts_series
    filename    = "input/{domain}/{grid_name}_climate.nc"
    name        = "t2m"
    units_in    = "K"
    units_out   = "degC"
    unit_scale  = 1.0
    unit_offset = -273.15
    with_time   = .TRUE.
    time_par    = -22000.0, 0.0, 1.0, 12.0    ! x0, x1, dx, n_sub (monthly)
/
```

## Usage example

```fortran
use varslice, only : varslice_class, varslice_init_nml, varslice_update

type(varslice_class) :: vs
real(wp) :: time_now

! 1. Configure the variable from a namelist group (loads coords + time axis).
call varslice_init_nml(vs, "input_par.nml", group="ts_series", &
                       domain="Greenland", grid_name="GRL-16KM")

! 2. Update to a given time. Here: a 12-month climatology (mean over a
!    2000-year window, applied to every 12th sub-annual index).
time_now = -8000.0_wp
call varslice_update(vs, time=[time_now-1000.0_wp, time_now+1000.0_wp], &
                     method="range_mean", rep=12, print_summary=.TRUE.)

! 3. Use the result. vs%var is rank-4 (x, y, z, time); here z=1, time=1..12.
!    e.g. the January field:
!        vs%var(:,:,1,1)
!    or a single interpolated snapshot instead:
call varslice_update(vs, time=[time_now], method="interp")
!        vs%var(:,:,1,1)   ! interpolated 2-D field at time_now
```

## See also

- [series](series.qmd) — 0-D time-series reader (the non-gridded analogue)
- [ncio](ncio.qmd) — the underlying netCDF read/write layer
- [coords](coords.qmd) — grids and mapping used by `varslice_map_to_grid`
